Documentation for this format

Options Sets

Answer options for overview.sponsor

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-sponsor-cs#medical-operations ("Medical Operations")

Answer options for inventory-check.kit-type

  • Crew Medical Restraint System (CMRS)
  • Ambulatory Medical Pack (AMP)
  • Crew Health Care System (CHeCS)
  • Advanced Life Support Pack (ALSP)
  • Dental Kit
  • Eye Kit
  • IV Kit
  • Minor Surgery Kit

Answer options for equipment.defibrillator-status

  • Functional - tested
  • Needs maintenance
  • Non-functional
  • N/A

Answer options for resupply.priority

  • Urgent - next available vehicle
  • High - within 60 days
  • Normal - next planned resupply
  • Low - can wait

Answer options for summary.overall-status

  • Adequate - no action needed
  • Minor shortages - monitor
  • Significant gaps - resupply needed
  • Critical - immediate action required

Answer options for data-delivery.archive

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-data-archive-cs#mmis ("Medical Mission Information System")

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MR036LMedicalKitInventory
title: MR036L Medical Kit Inventory
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for medical kit inventory management. Tracks medical supplies, equipment status, and expiration dates aboard ISS.
purpose: To maintain accurate inventory of medical supplies and equipment aboard ISS, track expiration dates, and ensure adequate supplies for crew health.
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To maintain accurate inventory of medical supplies, track expiration dates, and ensure crew has adequate medical resources.
